name: Greisa, United We Dream Action
subject: We refuse to give up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United We Dream Action; UnitedWeDreamAction
body:
You can help create a strong network of support at a time when it's needed most. | | | Yesterday, Donald Trump was sworn in as President of the United States for a second time. As expected, Trump began his first day announcing a slate of violent, anti-immigrant executive orders to fuel his administration’s destructive and vile plans for mass raids, family separations, detentions, and deportations. Among them, Trump announced he will unleash the military against families seeking safety at the border, target immigrants all across the country for mass deportations, and reinstate heinous policies like Remain in Mexico, while also attempting to attack the 14th Amendment. **It’s crucial that we meet this dark moment in history with defiance and dissent.** At United We Dream Action, we know that our work to empower and share the stories of immigrant youth is _vitally important_ to our future. Mass deportations will tear the fabric of our communities and cause incalculable harm to immigrant families. Every day people will be impacted by Trump’s attacks — your child’s DACAmented teacher, the abuela who lives down the street — so we cannot wait on the sidelines until our representatives take action on our behalf. **[We’re galvanizing our momentum NOW and your support will help us build a movement against the Trump administration backed by the individual voices of our community. clean_body: You can help create a strong network of support at a time when it's needed most. | | | Yesterday, Donald Trump was sworn in as President of the United States for a second time. As expected, Trump began his first day announcing a slate of violent, anti-immigrant executive orders to fuel his administration’s destructive and vile plans for mass raids, family separations, detentions, and deportations. Among them, Trump announced he will unleash the military against families seeking safety at the border, target immigrants all across the country for mass deportations, and reinstate heinous policies like Remain in Mexico, while also attempting to attack the 14th Amendment. It’s crucial that we meet this dark moment in history with defiance and dissent. At United We Dream Action, we know that our work to empower and share the stories of immigrant youth is vitally important to our future. Mass deportations will tear the fabric of our communities and cause incalculable harm to immigrant families. Every day people will be impacted by Trump’s attacks — your child’s DACAmented teacher, the abuela who lives down the street — so we cannot wait on the sidelines until our representatives take action on our behalf. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Contributions to United We Dream Action are not tax deductible. The respective merchants of record for transactions to United We Dream Action are ActBlue Civics Inc. They receive and process those donations and subsequently give them to the organization or cause indicated on the contribution form within 30 days. During an extremely difficult time for millions of immigrants, you can help create a strong network of support at a time when it's needed most. Thank you for standing with us today. Greisa, United We Dream Action Follow us on: | | | | | Paid for by United We Dream Action; UnitedWeDreamAction.org; Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Team AOC
subject: Trump & the GND
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
"We will end the Green New Deal..."
#
Yesterday, Donald Trump was inaugurated as 47th President of the United States.
In his inaugural address, Trump said, "With my actions today, we will end the Green New Deal" — **and he wasted no time keeping that promise:**
********
President Trump said we will "export American energy all over the world. We will be a rich nation, and **it is that liquid gold under our feet that will help to do it**."
Trump's inaugural promise comes on the heels of the hottest year in history.
From the air we breathe to the water we drink — **when we fight for the Green New Deal, we are fighting for a liveable planet for future generations.**
Now, we need to be ready to fight back like never before.
[Will you donate $5 right now to power Alexandria's fight for bold action in response to our climate crisis? Every dollar makes a difference in our campaign for real progress.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250121.IGyrur&refcode=em20250121-7955-P_FND_RES_GND&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=7955%2E2526796%2Et0CiCP)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250121.IGyrur&refcode=em20250121-7955-P_FND_RES_GND&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=7955%2E2526796%2Et0CiCP)**
Whether or not you can give today, thank you so much for being a part of this movement.
In solidarity,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250121?refcode=em20250121-7955-P_FND_RES_GND&t=21&refcode2=7955_2526796_t0CiCP&akid=7955%2E2526796%2Et0CiCP)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250121-7955-P_FND_RES_GND&t=22&akid=7955%2E2526796%2Et0CiCP)**

* **TOP NEWS**

"**A rare winter storm is bringing snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast,** " AP's**Sarah Brumfield** -- "A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana.

The storm [is expected to scatter heavy snow](https://substack.com/redirect/09e6d13d-20c5-41b6-b158-4fa1d4495a81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), sleet and freezing rain around the Deep South as a blast of Arctic air plunges much of the eastern U.S. into a deep freeze.

Nearly 2,000 flights within the U.S. or entering or leaving the country were canceled Tuesday with about 10,000 more flights delayed, according to online tracker FlightAware.com. In Texas, both Houston airports said that flight operations would be suspended starting Tuesday in expectation of hazardous conditions.

The East Coast endured a thick blanket of snow while people from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine shivered in [bitterly cold temperatures](https://substack.com/redirect/fde3186d-b2d0-411c-9fad-477d075133de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the frigid arctic air mass that sent temperatures plunging well below normal. Dangerously cold wind chills were expected to persist through Tuesday morning.

**Lakesha Reed** , manager of Beaucoup Eats catering in New Orleans, had plans to fly out Tuesday to cook for a Mardi Gras-style event in the nation's capital, but flights were canceled amid extreme cold. The 47-year-old New Orleans native said it was in the 30s early Monday afternoon in her port city, where near-freezing temperatures are rare.

"We can barely drive in the rain," she said. "Last year, we wore shorts for Mardi Gras."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/22297d71-7544-4bf6-b551-955a3784207f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Winter storm warnings extended from Texas to North Carolina on Tuesday, with heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain expected to move eastward through the region into Wednesday. Meanwhile, a state of emergency was declared Monday night across at least a dozen counties in New York as heavy [lake-effect snow](https://substack.com/redirect/73e42612-5c96-47b7-9313-eeeff1bc2ede?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was expected around Lake Ontario and Lake Erie through Wednesday -- with 1 to 2 feet possible -- along with extreme cold temperatures.

Ahead of the storm, governors in Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama, declared states of emergency and many school systems canceled classes Tuesday.

The blizzard warning in effect until midday Tuesday was the first issued by the office in Lake Charles, Louisiana, according to meteorologist **Donald Jones**. Strong winds with heavier snow bands were reducing visibility, and areas across the Gulf South that rarely see snow are expecting near-record or record snowfall, Jones said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/22297d71-7544-4bf6-b551-955a3784207f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"The last time we saw snow of this magnitude was way back in 1960, and prior to that, the previous snowfall record that even stands to this day was way back in 1895," Jones said. "By modern standards this is going to be a historic and very memorable storm for this part of the world."

In Harahan, a suburb of New Orleans, as a rare snowstorm began to cover the roads, a Harahan police officer without gloves rubbed his hands to warm them, after responding to a church security alarm. Sleet changed to snow as the sun rose, with scarcely a car on the road.

Before snow and sleet began falling Monday night, Houston Mayor **John Whitmire** asked residents to stay off the roads.

"Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours," he said noting that temperatures weren't expected to get above freezing until Thursday. "So don't let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast."

Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday.

Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/ca217e74-4ec0-4848-922c-631d6e2aab1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

"Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours," he said noting that temperatures weren't expected to get above freezing until Thursday. "So don't let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast." Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday. Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." Hakeem Jeffries is the Democratic Leader of the U.S. House of Representatives. Like many of us, Hakeem was raised by a working-class family; His mom was a social worker and his dad was a substance abuse counselor.

Now, as the top Democrat in the House, he’s fighting to stop MAGA extremism and reclaim the majority for Democrats.

_" If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator **Phil Gramm** (R-TX)_
* * *
**AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED**
Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to [ATX Pulse](https://substack.com/redirect/294c0b77-6860-4ee4-b428-2c85d4a70029?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/42d528be-b455-4f8c-8ec8-e110dc3113ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here:_ [ATXpulsepremium.com](https://substack.com/redirect/36ab732f-3e4c-41bb-960b-d118a99479e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**A rare winter storm is bringing snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast,** " AP's**Sarah Brumfield** -- "A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana.

The storm [is expected to scatter heavy snow](https://substack.com/redirect/1ae52425-448a-4673-baff-c1dfe0a55cba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), sleet and freezing rain around the Deep South as a blast of Arctic air plunges much of the eastern U.S. into a deep freeze.

Nearly 2,000 flights within the U.S. or entering or leaving the country were canceled Tuesday with about 10,000 more flights delayed, according to online tracker FlightAware.com. In Texas, both Houston airports said that flight operations would be suspended starting Tuesday in expectation of hazardous conditions.

The East Coast endured a thick blanket of snow while people from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine shivered in [bitterly cold temperatures](https://substack.com/redirect/7d34a0ae-f083-4f59-b744-4fddeb0f5ac5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the frigid arctic air mass that sent temperatures plunging well below normal. Dangerously cold wind chills were expected to persist through Tuesday morning.

**Lakesha Reed** , manager of Beaucoup Eats catering in New Orleans, had plans to fly out Tuesday to cook for a Mardi Gras-style event in the nation's capital, but flights were canceled amid extreme cold. The 47-year-old New Orleans native said it was in the 30s early Monday afternoon in her port city, where near-freezing temperatures are rare.

"We can barely drive in the rain," she said. "Last year, we wore shorts for Mardi Gras."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/58720087-7f22-47d6-93ea-a784c27cfba7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Winter storm warnings extended from Texas to North Carolina on Tuesday, with heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain expected to move eastward through the region into Wednesday. Meanwhile, a state of emergency was declared Monday night across at least a dozen counties in New York as heavy [lake-effect snow](https://substack.com/redirect/4ad186ae-10f3-4b71-a93f-7b865bd293b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was expected around Lake Ontario and Lake Erie through Wednesday -- with 1 to 2 feet possible -- along with extreme cold temperatures.

Ahead of the storm, governors in Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama, declared states of emergency and many school systems canceled classes Tuesday.

The blizzard warning in effect until midday Tuesday was the first issued by the office in Lake Charles, Louisiana, according to meteorologist **Donald Jones**. Strong winds with heavier snow bands were reducing visibility, and areas across the Gulf South that rarely see snow are expecting near-record or record snowfall, Jones said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/58720087-7f22-47d6-93ea-a784c27cfba7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"The last time we saw snow of this magnitude was way back in 1960, and prior to that, the previous snowfall record that even stands to this day was way back in 1895," Jones said. "By modern standards this is going to be a historic and very memorable storm for this part of the world."

In Harahan, a suburb of New Orleans, as a rare snowstorm began to cover the roads, a Harahan police officer without gloves rubbed his hands to warm them, after responding to a church security alarm. Sleet changed to snow as the sun rose, with scarcely a car on the road.

Before snow and sleet began falling Monday night, Houston Mayor **John Whitmire** asked residents to stay off the roads.

"Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours," he said noting that temperatures weren't expected to get above freezing until Thursday. "So don't let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast."

Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday.

Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/3e652f7b-ebba-4a64-b51c-d78e00799eb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED** _: As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries._

KHOU: "**Bush, Hobby Airports remained closed Tuesday due to snow, sleet** " [KHOU](https://substack.com/redirect/f4188364-5aba-4374-82bb-e2547a88a7ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **#TXLEGE**

TX TRIB: "**Texas Democrats hope to benefit after boosting Dustin Burrows to House speaker** " [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/b2533211-50fc-4ac7-a6fe-2e2b89671916?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **STATE GOVERNMENT**

BLOOMBERG WIRE: "**Extreme cold and record snowfall to test Texas power grid** " [Bloomberg Wire](https://substack.com/redirect/d3af81d5-7a95-46ad-8270-cdcf7f2688ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **BUSINESS NEWS**

AAS: "**2.6M Tesla vehicles under investigation due to Actually Smart Summon feature** " [AAS](https://substack.com/redirect/765b8a84-cae7-413f-ada7-b0c3dcb48d55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
BLOOMBERG WIRE: "**Trump plans to enact 25% tariffs on Mexico, Canada on Feb. 1** " [Bloomberg Wire](https://substack.com/redirect/d36ef0e3-4924-41be-bc07-99fdbefbe485?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *

Thank you for being a paid subscriber. " If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed." - Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | | ---|---|--- Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . TOP NEWS "A rare winter storm is bringing snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast, " AP'sSarah Brumfield -- "A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana. The storm , sleet and freezing rain around the Deep South as a blast of Arctic air plunges much of the eastern U.S. into a deep freeze. Nearly 2,000 flights within the U.S. or entering or leaving the country were canceled Tuesday with about 10,000 more flights delayed, according to online tracker FlightAware.com. In Texas, both Houston airports said that flight operations would be suspended starting Tuesday in expectation of hazardous conditions. The East Coast endured a thick blanket of snow while people from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine shivered in from the frigid arctic air mass that sent temperatures plunging well below normal. Dangerously cold wind chills were expected to persist through Tuesday morning. Lakesha Reed , manager of Beaucoup Eats catering in New Orleans, had plans to fly out Tuesday to cook for a Mardi Gras-style event in the nation's capital, but flights were canceled amid extreme cold. The 47-year-old New Orleans native said it was in the 30s early Monday afternoon in her port city, where near-freezing temperatures are rare. "We can barely drive in the rain," she said. "Last year, we wore shorts for Mardi Gras." Winter storm warnings extended from Texas to North Carolina on Tuesday, with heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain expected to move eastward through the region into Wednesday. Meanwhile, a state of emergency was declared Monday night across at least a dozen counties in New York as heavy was expected around Lake Ontario and Lake Erie through Wednesday -- with 1 to 2 feet possible -- along with extreme cold temperatures. Ahead of the storm, governors in Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama, declared states of emergency and many school systems canceled classes Tuesday. The blizzard warning in effect until midday Tuesday was the first issued by the office in Lake Charles, Louisiana, according to meteorologist Donald Jones. Strong winds with heavier snow bands were reducing visibility, and areas across the Gulf South that rarely see snow are expecting near-record or record snowfall, Jones said. "The last time we saw snow of this magnitude was way back in 1960, and prior to that, the previous snowfall record that even stands to this day was way back in 1895," Jones said. "By modern standards this is going to be a historic and very memorable storm for this part of the world." In Harahan, a suburb of New Orleans, as a rare snowstorm began to cover the roads, a Harahan police officer without gloves rubbed his hands to warm them, after responding to a church security alarm. Sleet changed to snow as the sun rose, with scarcely a car on the road. Before snow and sleet began falling Monday night, Houston Mayor John Whitmire asked residents to stay off the roads. "Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours," he said noting that temperatures weren't expected to get above freezing until Thursday. "So don't let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast." Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday. Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." clean_body: | = | | Elon Musk, the world’s richest man and Donald Trump’s biggest benefactor, is targeting me and other House Democrats by name, Peter: = | | | = It’s bad enough that Musk poured more than a quarter of a billion dollars into electing Trump, but now he’s influencing policy and threatening to use his fortune to buy MORE elections. This oligarchy runs counter to everything our country stands for, Peter, and you’d better believe I won’t be backing down in the House. I work for Kentuckians and their families. Not for Trump, and not for Elon Musk. * **TOP NEWS**

“**A rare winter storm is bringing snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast,** ” AP's**Sarah Brumfield** — “A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana.

The storm [is expected to scatter heavy snow](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.taRaxrEkWqxIZl3CYy7RU-8zPWZErbzsTpoOJuc75r1F5o9XHaLcSyO8X1u9Y6tuUkj3GwrSZ2LejGx7Xyn6bd6AEMZghd_vh35LAqqB4rs34sriDr3CsjgCTlcFpa7oAWdO4_hrDERtVgVUj0OHxH1MkamPEouOdQPEjYhGr9g6aRrw8k_5IowF9PTKtWL8XLgHnCl-NJtw5sVH5LlWEPg7cLVkInM_ofGvVIkWPLGVIwk0-3oPebcT_QekaPQcJsd0aKCL5ZVjzdTZw9lpkzJSWf2CqKeV8XhfyFzFlVjgrEC1IMJXx16zM9xU3v82R2iNoegjEprQsggqBLge5lCxvFDNBN_vQ6wQdt7Wssx88V4kWY9HsV9TYPJUZOMTdaLS2rndK0ASfzLCJxqeXqCvfpq6jNmYl7rVOeOrJpRhp7QyVPfReeceAl84mgj5Jn41g0au-W-ZZjLrp5XcSEEzm6VfzliYhXHeE5kVHg8/4dc/ZXJX-moJTYCc7EMZMyTUTQ/h7/h001.5ir8td0MoOme7QVbfIezmuE9A-AFNTYy_E-5e6tK_uA), sleet and freezing rain around the Deep South as a blast of Arctic air plunges much of the eastern U.S. into a deep freeze.

Nearly 2,000 flights within the U.S. or entering or leaving the country were canceled Tuesday with about 10,000 more flights delayed, according to online tracker FlightAware.com. In Texas, both Houston airports said that flight operations would be suspended starting Tuesday in expectation of hazardous conditions.

The East Coast endured a thick blanket of snow while people from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine shivered in [bitterly cold temperatures](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.taRaxrEkWqxIZl3CYy7RU1UCn2-1t8bKAxYr5PYf6FyaYBOYRAEtpe_zpitalPbMSE0zHQkZmh91rrlVeFCkVVF38yzNnCdKV-UxULxys4b8A8pWJyzoqzjPamKCTCNtftzMYCEQ_2gXawC_Vx5S-lfnw0-kTigZN7UV00nmzrZmV2QdkftxDVK9lMj9jUzd-XBXwXj6HRE_Gz4Cw4CNvS_TXPcA0LUetfbXWBCzundtv1OoAsRS_rp5o9MOkT5ZDa0iRjVjlT_QEvwG4hzXYM15T3ZTauM_dbOG9zcoDGwp4Ke2TOGzIOSbINmFAVI2EWG4Nb8brxTKMvAjJma_ecAVxFX0WrNaulw1Y1o3ExMRmnsAU0Q07be5i9C3FebfpnhqB_yy5oCbpi1w4zV48Zvhwh4d-PW-HWIzqBWg4RQ/4dc/ZXJX-moJTYCc7EMZMyTUTQ/h8/h001.NUTvltJuDa1jyIRi4UwLYmGutSE6x9yIMra9hrs2yVQ) from the frigid arctic air mass that sent temperatures plunging well below normal. Dangerously cold wind chills were expected to persist through Tuesday morning.

**Lakesha Reed** , manager of Beaucoup Eats catering in New Orleans, had plans to fly out Tuesday to cook for a Mardi Gras-style event in the nation’s capital, but flights were canceled amid extreme cold. The 47-year-old New Orleans native said it was in the 30s early Monday afternoon in her port city, where near-freezing temperatures are rare.

“We can barely drive in the rain,” she said. “Last year, we wore shorts for Mardi Gras.”

Winter storm warnings extended from Texas to North Carolina on Tuesday, with heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain expected to move eastward through the region into Wednesday. Meanwhile, a state of emergency was declared Monday night across at least a dozen counties in New York as heavy [lake-effect snow](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.taRaxrEkWqxIZl3CYy7RU-8zPWZErbzsTpoOJuc75r2rsuHeERG31B4sitvQaiimXjhg24sQOwCkiYWKB7GlptVTwnL0eVjwB8skxXOZYX1r9Z_mGdvyBE0qJKafmIr7yM_KuPtHM4G2M55vTGy8f-NHnnHvHLWubb__6CZtDWOGcQSdLJXt0tU_8KwTtE8ORdvlUTG3mY1MfxdB9MJup87MCLJMYe_5RUImo1gz20U685qriC-ovztv0uVg57s33-Jp30wG9y3_cx6BbFFtxhO4V-eYPzd_AIicrsBYG-03VzI9XseJIegQ8CstySuRVfyPz0k116WiIqrNv5-lBRi4l_768jZB_01dq9FO7MhKzgZfVDZNg_EuS0RB9qcIiZaMMFaFWLzQi3ewD4xl_FCksF7TDgnR1569hncpdf0gG63Holw6kWcBlsxqut47MK1_gCm2ThbypN-zEqUqZzl2iVGWn_j1IVhKG8JrWus/4dc/ZXJX-moJTYCc7EMZMyTUTQ/h10/h001.t3vtGAcP1GKJd8jL7Lj3ay5mG4V_Ua7e-cDzq1gX-Mk) was expected around Lake Ontario and Lake Erie through Wednesday — with 1 to 2 feet possible — along with extreme cold temperatures.

Ahead of the storm, governors in Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama, declared states of emergency and many school systems canceled classes Tuesday.

The blizzard warning in effect until midday Tuesday was the first issued by the office in Lake Charles, Louisiana, according to meteorologist **Donald Jones**. Strong winds with heavier snow bands were reducing visibility, and areas across the Gulf South that rarely see snow are expecting near-record or record snowfall, Jones said.

“The last time we saw snow of this magnitude was way back in 1960, and prior to that, the previous snowfall record that even stands to this day was way back in 1895,” Jones said. “By modern standards this is going to be a historic and very memorable storm for this part of the world.”

In Harahan, a suburb of New Orleans, as a rare snowstorm began to cover the roads, a Harahan police officer without gloves rubbed his hands to warm them, after responding to a church security alarm. Sleet changed to snow as the sun rose, with scarcely a car on the road.

Before snow and sleet began falling Monday night, Houston Mayor **John Whitmire** asked residents to stay off the roads.

“Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours,” he said noting that temperatures weren’t expected to get above freezing until Thursday. “So don’t let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast.”

Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday.

Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." Here's What You Need to Know in Texas Today. | | – BY: | TUESDAY || 1/21/25 | Good Tuesday afternoon. Thank you for being a paid subscriber. | “If something of importance is known in Texas, Matt knows it. With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed.” – Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) | AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . | TOP NEWS “A rare winter storm is bringing snow, sleet and ice to Texas and the northern Gulf Coast, ” AP'sSarah Brumfield — “A rare winter storm charging through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday has closed highways and airports and prompted the first blizzard warning for southeast Texas and southwest Louisiana. The storm , sleet and freezing rain around the Deep South as a blast of Arctic air plunges much of the eastern U.S. into a deep freeze. Nearly 2,000 flights within the U.S. or entering or leaving the country were canceled Tuesday with about 10,000 more flights delayed, according to online tracker FlightAware.com. In Texas, both Houston airports said that flight operations would be suspended starting Tuesday in expectation of hazardous conditions. The East Coast endured a thick blanket of snow while people from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine shivered in from the frigid arctic air mass that sent temperatures plunging well below normal. Dangerously cold wind chills were expected to persist through Tuesday morning. Lakesha Reed , manager of Beaucoup Eats catering in New Orleans, had plans to fly out Tuesday to cook for a Mardi Gras-style event in the nation’s capital, but flights were canceled amid extreme cold. The 47-year-old New Orleans native said it was in the 30s early Monday afternoon in her port city, where near-freezing temperatures are rare. “We can barely drive in the rain,” she said. “Last year, we wore shorts for Mardi Gras.” » Winter storm warnings extended from Texas to North Carolina on Tuesday, with heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain expected to move eastward through the region into Wednesday. Meanwhile, a state of emergency was declared Monday night across at least a dozen counties in New York as heavy was expected around Lake Ontario and Lake Erie through Wednesday — with 1 to 2 feet possible — along with extreme cold temperatures. Ahead of the storm, governors in Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi and Alabama, declared states of emergency and many school systems canceled classes Tuesday. The blizzard warning in effect until midday Tuesday was the first issued by the office in Lake Charles, Louisiana, according to meteorologist Donald Jones. Strong winds with heavier snow bands were reducing visibility, and areas across the Gulf South that rarely see snow are expecting near-record or record snowfall, Jones said. » “The last time we saw snow of this magnitude was way back in 1960, and prior to that, the previous snowfall record that even stands to this day was way back in 1895,” Jones said. “By modern standards this is going to be a historic and very memorable storm for this part of the world.” In Harahan, a suburb of New Orleans, as a rare snowstorm began to cover the roads, a Harahan police officer without gloves rubbed his hands to warm them, after responding to a church security alarm. Sleet changed to snow as the sun rose, with scarcely a car on the road. Before snow and sleet began falling Monday night, Houston Mayor John Whitmire asked residents to stay off the roads. “Get prepared to be in your house, in your residence, in your warming place for the next 48 hours,” he said noting that temperatures weren’t expected to get above freezing until Thursday. “So don’t let even clear skies on Wednesday and other days fool you. This is a serious arctic blast.” Snowfall rates of 1 inch per hour or more were possible from eastern Texas to the western Florida panhandle and historic snowfall of 3 to 6 inches was forecast along the Interstate 10 corridor including Houston; New Orleans; Mobile, Alabama; and Pensacola, Florida, according to the National Weather Service. Heavy snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected across Georgia, northern Florida and the eastern Carolinas from Tuesday night into Wednesday. Forecasters warned the subfreezing morning lows could threaten sensitive vegetation and exposed plumbing in areas unaccustomed to bitter cold." body:
As you’ve most likely seen, parts of beautiful Southern California are being destroyed by ruthless flames.

| [ ](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkD-P3CAQRz8N7mzxxwZcUJy02e5SRCelRLMw5x0FbATs3tePnFyU-tp5mie9389X_wqUaN--Q0bXa_e1eUCfYPygFN-p4hgJCtbmJZfLyMUohVdDdLORUekBnTDKzOssBB_uLlrL5U3b28rhXWu5WGNQmcXMVs9hFQO508OFFGKdreST1CANGouLui2IwGYOCWtvU79jxfK4JQqwd4Q8hSMPyd17L42pFyavTF43euKU6IkQOh37dNSNyWv4H9A-C8ZY6Ynnk7o-evbteNSATF1KPVrB0GnfmNQnyhjpkZm6YAZKn8cAuQBtO1OXv0JfIPz6B4-9496ZuiRK4F--jW8_3jS3fB2qi-WDUqKmDZv5dir_lPSvDP508ncAAAD__x3DjHg)

#
Friend of Life,

As you've most likely seen, parts of beautiful Southern California are being destroyed by ruthless flames.

My family and I are safe, but many are not. As we pray for the protection of all those in the fire's path, there is something else you can do to help these victims.

Live Action is partnering with EveryLife to send diapers to families impacted by the fires. From now through the end of the month, every gift of $50, using this link, will enable a box of diapers (6 packs!) to be sent to a family in critical need. Will you step up now for these families? [ ](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkD-P3CAQRz8N7mzxxwZcUJy02e5SRCelRLMw5x0FbATs3tePnFyU-tp5mie9389X_wqUaN--Q0bXa_e1eUCfYPygFN-p4hgJCtbmJZfLyMUohVdDdLORUekBnTDKzOssBB_uLlrL5U3b28rhXWu5WGNQmcXMVs9hFQO508OFFGKdreST1CANGouLui2IwGYOCWtvU79jxfK4JQqwd4Q8hSMPyd17L42pFyavTF43euKU6IkQOh37dNSNyWv4H9A-C8ZY6Ynnk7o-evbteNSATF1KPVrB0GnfmNQnyhjpkZm6YAZKn8cAuQBtO1OXv0JfIPz6B4-9496ZuiRK4F--jW8_3jS3fB2qi-WDUqKmDZv5dir_lPSvDP508ncAAAD__x3DjHg)
With 5 already dead and over 2,000 buildings destroyed, news reports are saying this is one of the most destructive fires in recorded history for this region of California.

Over 130,000 have been evacuated so far and many are struggling to meet basic needs, unsure what they'll be returning to after the flames subside.

In these stressful moments, practical necessities make all the difference, especially when babies are involved. That's why we're partnering with EveryLife to provide diapers to families in need.

EveryLife is coordinating with CityServe and Convoy of Hope, working tirelessly to ensure the diapers reach California families in need by any means necessary.

This means all you need to do is fill out the donation form with a gift of $50 or more, and the rest will be taken care of for you.

Send a box containing 6 packs of diapers to a family in need: **[https://give.liveaction.org/ca-wildfires-diaper-drive/](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkD-P3CAQRz8N7mzxxwZcUJy02e5SRCelRLMw5x0FbATs3tePnFyU-tp5mie9389X_wqUaN--Q0bXa_e1eUCfYPygFN-p4hgJCtbmJZfLyMUohVdDdLORUekBnTDKzOssBB_uLlrL5U3b28rhXWu5WGNQmcXMVs9hFQO508OFFGKdreST1CANGouLui2IwGYOCWtvU79jxfK4JQqwd4Q8hSMPyd17L42pFyavTF43euKU6IkQOh37dNSNyWv4H9A-C8ZY6Ynnk7o-evbteNSATF1KPVrB0GnfmNQnyhjpkZm6YAZKn8cAuQBtO1OXv0JfIPz6B4-9496ZuiRK4F--jW8_3jS3fB2qi-WDUqKmDZv5dir_lPSvDP508ncAAAD__x3DjHg)**

Thank you for standing with us to help children and families affected during this critical time.

For Life,

Lila Rose
**Founder and President**

[](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkD-P3CAQRz8N7mzxxwZcUJy02e5SRCelRLMw5x0FbATs3tePnFyU-tp5mie9389X_wqUaN--Q0bXa_e1eUCfYPygFN-p4hgJCtbmJZfLyMUohVdDdLORUekBnTDKzOssBB_uLlrL5U3b28rhXWu5WGNQmcXMVs9hFQO508OFFGKdreST1CANGouLui2IwGYOCWtvU79jxfK4JQqwd4Q8hSMPyd17L42pFyavTF43euKU6IkQOh37dNSNyWv4H9A-C8ZY6Ynnk7o-evbteNSATF1KPVrB0GnfmNQnyhjpkZm6YAZKn8cAuQBtO1OXv0JfIPz6B4-9496ZuiRK4F--jW8_3jS3fB2qi-WDUqKmDZv5dir_lPSvDP508ncAAAD__x3DjHg)

2200 Wilson Blvd, Suite 102, PMB 111 Arlington, VA 22201

The Republican Team

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: The future of our state
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE
body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/5?t=1&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)
Team,
Do you know what gives me hope for the future of Michigan? **Our youth.**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250121-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250121-1&refcode2=43_181023_860AOU&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)
Fighting for the future of Michigan begins with us.
The next generation is already taking action — whether it’s volunteering in the community, running for local office, or voting in the last election. They are engaged citizens, and we need to uplift their voices as they continue to grow.
Our youth are the future, and I can see that they want to see the best for our state.
**[With that, I am doing everything that I can to empower our youth to keep fighting for our future. However, I can’t do it without you. Will you chip in $5 today to power my effort?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250121-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250121-1&refcode2=43_181023_860AOU&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250121-1?amount=5&refcode=em20250121-1&refcode2=43_181023_860AOU&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)**
Thank you for your continued support.
— Jocelyn

* * *
Before you go... we need your help. Since her 2018 election, Secretary Benson has fought for free and fair elections. She’s on the front lines of defending and strengthening our democracy. **[Will you contribute today to show your support?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250121-1?refcode=em20250121-1&refcode2=43_181023_860AOU&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)** **[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250121-1?refcode=em20250121-1&refcode2=43_181023_860AOU&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)**
---

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_2024/?t=3&akid=43%2E181023%2E860AOU)

Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State
PO BOX 21368,
Detroit, MI 48221
PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE

name: JanePAC
subject: Trump’s first act in office? Destroy the planet.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
From abandoning the Paris Agreement to gutting clean energy, Trump’s attack has started.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100751882/523767296/1328794144?refcode=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ms=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1ZTU1NzM2LTMxZDgtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3LMlP7nmTK6nylB8Oq2Hpvwrn5YI13VGPhPjoDJWRgA=&emci=6fc7fe6e-20d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=4823105)
---
Yesterday, Donald Trump showed us exactly what he meant when he vowed to be “ _a dictator on day one_.” Within hours of taking office, he unleashed a blizzard of executive orders aimed at destroying progress on climate, dismantling American freedoms, and consolidating power in ways that should terrify all of us. **[Trump’s assault on our democracy and environment has begun, but so has our resistance. Rush a donation to support leaders who will fight for climate justice and protect our communities from this administration’s deadly agenda.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100751883/523767297/2105508926?refcode=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ms=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1ZTU1NzM2LTMxZDgtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3LMlP7nmTK6nylB8Oq2Hpvwrn5YI13VGPhPjoDJWRgA=)** Here are some of the more egregious executive orders Trump signed yesterday:
* **Withdrew from the Paris Climate Agreement**. Once again, Trump isolated the U.S. from nearly 200 nations working together to combat the climate crisis, abandoning the global fight to curb emissions and protect our planet.
* **Opened public lands and waters to fossil fuel drilling**. Trump greenlit oil drilling and mining in some of the most fragile, irreplaceable wilderness areas — including Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
* **Attacked clean energy progress**. He aimed to repeal rules that promote electric vehicles and initiated a halt to leasing federal waters for wind farms, throwing obstacles in the way of renewable energy.
* **Slashed protections for vulnerable communities**. Trump ordered the elimination of government offices and programs designed to protect low-income and frontline communities from the devastating impacts of pollution and climate disasters.

And then, in true Trump fashion, he declared a national “energy emergency” because it gives him sweeping powers to bulldoze pipelines, fast-track fossil fuel projects, and dismantle clean energy progress. Let’s call this what it is: **authoritarianism in action.** Declaring fake “emergencies” to hand power to his corporate donors and silence opposition is straight out of the dictator playbook. And Trump’s allies in Big Oil are celebrating and popping champagne while the rest of us face the consequences. We’ve pushed back against Trump’s dangerous policies in the past, and now we’re ready to take him on again — but we need your help to fuel the fight. **[Will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can to help us combat Trump’s destructive agenda, elect climate champions across the country, and build a firewall of state and local climate policies to protect our communities?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100751884/523767298/2105508926?refcode=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ms=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1ZTU1NzM2LTMxZDgtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3LMlP7nmTK6nylB8Oq2Hpvwrn5YI13VGPhPjoDJWRgA=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [**DONATE $10**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100751885/523767299/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ms=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1ZTU1NzM2LTMxZDgtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3LMlP7nmTK6nylB8Oq2Hpvwrn5YI13VGPhPjoDJWRgA=) | [**DONATE $25**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100751886/523767300/2105508926?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ms=ea.d5e55736-31d8-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ1ZTU1NzM2LTMxZDgtZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=3LMlP7nmTK6nylB8Oq2Hpvwrn5YI13VGPhPjoDJWRgA=)

clean_body: From abandoning the Paris Agreement to gutting clean energy, Trump’s attack has started. | Yesterday, Donald Trump showed us exactly what he meant when he vowed to be “ a dictator on day one.” Within hours of taking office, he unleashed a blizzard of executive orders aimed at destroying progress on climate, dismantling American freedoms, and consolidating power in ways that should terrify all of us. = Here are some of the more egregious executive orders Trump signed yesterday: Withdrew from the Paris Climate Agreement. Once again, Trump isolated the U.S. from nearly 200 nations working together to combat the climate crisis, abandoning the global fight to curb emissions and protect our planet. Opened public lands and waters to fossil fuel drilling. Trump greenlit oil drilling and mining in some of the most fragile, irreplaceable wilderness areas — including Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge. Attacked clean energy progress. He aimed to repeal rules that promote electric vehicles and initiated a halt to leasing federal waters for wind farms, throwing obstacles in the way of renewable energy. Slashed protections for vulnerable communities. Trump ordered the elimination of government offices and programs designed to protect low-income and frontline communities from the devastating impacts of pollution and climate disasters. And then, in true Trump fashion, he declared a national “energy emergency” because it gives him sweeping powers to bulldoze pipelines, fast-track fossil fuel projects, and dismantle clean energy progress. Let’s call this what it is: authoritarianism in action. Declaring fake “emergencies” to hand power to his corporate donors and silence opposition is straight out of the dictator playbook. And Trump’s allies in Big Oil are celebrating and popping champagne while the rest of us face the consequences. We’ve pushed back against Trump’s dangerous policies in the past, and now we’re ready to take him on again — but we need your help to fuel the fight. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Onward, Team JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

#### "Realigning the United States Refugee Admission Program"
Trump uses baseless and conpiratorial attacks to justify his order that immediately freezes the United States Refugee Assistance Program (USRAP), which only assists those who have been or are in immediate risk of being persecuted in their home countries.
Trump begins Section I with a blatantly untrue statement, falsely claiming that the US was "inundated with record levels of migration" during the Biden administration.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/be10b764-0224-4015-bea7-c1f3a0621dbd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Not every immigrant is qualified to use the USRAP program. Data from the Department of State shows only 27,308 total admissions in 2024 under the USRAP, with the largest share coming from Eastern Asia.
The ceiling (meaning the cap for how many people from a region are allowed in via USRAP) for Africa alone was 50,000 in 2024, so this is by no means a program overwhelmed or strained.
The United States received refugees under the program from 85 different countries.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/124282a4-d5bd-4d39-b99b-824489136661?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The total unallocated reserve for the USRAP program - meaning the unfilled spaces between the number of people entering through USRAP versus the cap - was 125,000.
During Trump's first term, 118,202 refugees were admitted under USRAP.
Targeting the refugee program -- a drop in the bucket of total immigration and one that serves the most destitute people in the world -- is a vile, cruel act of hatred.
Fewer than 11 million people are undocumented immigrants of the nearly 50 million foreign-born Americans living in the United States. And those who are part of the USRAP are _not undocumented._
In another executive order, Trump orders an end to birthright citizenship, an unconstitutional offense which _should_ be soundly defeated even in this far-right Supreme Court.
"Protecting the meaning and value of American Citizenship" is to refuse citizenship to anyone whose mother was:
* "Unlawfully present" in the United States at time of birth, or
* Lawfully present on a temporary basis

[Austin Kocher](https://open.substack.com/users/20912231-austin-kocher?utm_source=mentions) was so kind as to clean up all of the executive orders related to immigration and [drop them here](https://substack.com/redirect/0759dc33-c670-4ba3-be20-a3e12f30e31f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

clean_body: "Realigning the United States Refugee Admission Program" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Public Post (free!) Jan 21 | | | | | | | | | | | "Realigning the United States Refugee Admission Program" Trump uses baseless and conpiratorial attacks to justify his order that immediately freezes the United States Refugee Assistance Program (USRAP), which only assists those who have been or are in immediate risk of being persecuted in their home countries. Trump begins Section I with a blatantly untrue statement, falsely claiming that the US was "inundated with record levels of migration" during the Biden administration. | | ---|---|--- Not every immigrant is qualified to use the USRAP program. Data from the Department of State shows only 27,308 total admissions in 2024 under the USRAP, with the largest share coming from Eastern Asia. The ceiling (meaning the cap for how many people from a region are allowed in via USRAP) for Africa alone was 50,000 in 2024, so this is by no means a program overwhelmed or strained. The United States received refugees under the program from 85 different countries. The total unallocated reserve for the USRAP program - meaning the unfilled spaces between the number of people entering through USRAP versus the cap - was 125,000. During Trump's first term, 118,202 refugees were admitted under USRAP. Targeting the refugee program -- a drop in the bucket of total immigration and one that serves the most destitute people in the world -- is a vile, cruel act of hatred. Fewer than 11 million people are undocumented immigrants of the nearly 50 million foreign-born Americans living in the United States. And those who are part of the USRAP are not undocumented. In another executive order, Trump orders an end to birthright citizenship, an unconstitutional offense which should be soundly defeated even in this far-right Supreme Court. "Protecting the meaning and value of American Citizenship" is to refuse citizenship to anyone whose mother was: "Unlawfully present" in the United States at time of birth, or Lawfully present on a temporary basis was so kind as to clean up all of the executive orders related to immigration and . subject: You can send diapers to the fire victims
body:
As you’ve most likely seen, parts of beautiful Southern California are being destroyed by ruthless flames.

| [ ](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/1WZGgFTJ145cM_N_0f5UgA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcpH2P0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9naXZlLmxpdmVhY3Rpb24ub3JnL2NhLXdpbGRmaXJlcy1kaWFwZXItZHJpdmUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9cHJvc3BlY3RpbmcmdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249ZGlhcGVyX3BhY2smdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9bGlsYV9BRS1ST042MDgxM1cDc3BjQgpnjpL-j2f5lm2iUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)

#
Friend of Life,

As you've most likely seen, parts of beautiful Southern California are being destroyed by ruthless flames.

My family and I are safe, but many are not. As we pray for the protection of all those in the fire's path, there is something else you can do to help these victims.

Live Action is partnering with EveryLife to send diapers to families impacted by the fires. From now through the end of the month, every gift of $50, using this link, will enable a box of diapers (6 packs!) to be sent to a family in critical need. Will you step up now for these families? [ ](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/1WZGgFTJ145cM_N_0f5UgA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcpH2P0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9naXZlLmxpdmVhY3Rpb24ub3JnL2NhLXdpbGRmaXJlcy1kaWFwZXItZHJpdmUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9cHJvc3BlY3RpbmcmdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249ZGlhcGVyX3BhY2smdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9bGlsYV9BRS1ST042MDgxM1cDc3BjQgpnjpL-j2f5lm2iUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)
With 5 already dead and over 2,000 buildings destroyed, news reports are saying this is one of the most destructive fires in recorded history for this region of California.

Over 130,000 have been evacuated so far and many are struggling to meet basic needs, unsure what they'll be returning to after the flames subside.

In these stressful moments, practical necessities make all the difference, especially when babies are involved. That's why we're partnering with EveryLife to provide diapers to families in need.

EveryLife is coordinating with CityServe and Convoy of Hope, working tirelessly to ensure the diapers reach California families in need by any means necessary.

This means all you need to do is fill out the donation form with a gift of $50 or more, and the rest will be taken care of for you.

Send a box containing 6 packs of diapers to a family in need: **[https://give.liveaction.org/ca-wildfires-diaper-drive/](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/1WZGgFTJ145cM_N_0f5UgA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcpH2P0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9naXZlLmxpdmVhY3Rpb24ub3JnL2NhLXdpbGRmaXJlcy1kaWFwZXItZHJpdmUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9cHJvc3BlY3RpbmcmdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249ZGlhcGVyX3BhY2smdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9bGlsYV9BRS1ST042MDgxM1cDc3BjQgpnjpL-j2f5lm2iUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)**

Thank you for standing with us to help children and families affected during this critical time.

For Life,

Lila Rose
**Founder and President**

[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/1WZGgFTJ145cM_N_0f5UgA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcpH2P0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9naXZlLmxpdmVhY3Rpb24ub3JnL2NhLXdpbGRmaXJlcy1kaWFwZXItZHJpdmUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9cHJvc3BlY3RpbmcmdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249ZGlhcGVyX3BhY2smdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9bGlsYV9BRS1ST042MDgxM1cDc3BjQgpnjpL-j2f5lm2iUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)

2200 Wilson Blvd, Suite 102, PMB 111 Arlington, VA 22201

Restore Our Nation

name: Sam W.
subject: He hates Gen Z - we hate him too (& will take him down)
body:
| |
---
I’m 24. Here’s what’s on my mind as Trump takes the oath of office. | [View in browser](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzI5MTc4MDI3LFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiN2U3OTc0MWU3ZTE3NTM1MiJ9)
| |
---
| [ ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzM1NDY5NTI5LFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiNGUyMGQxZWQ4ZGJmMWNlMSJ9)
---
|
---
Dear Friend,

My name is Sam Weinberg and I’ve spent **_the last 9 years of my life_** working to prevent the rise of Donald Trump and it’s for one simple reason: he threatens my future.

As a 15 year old, I volunteered for Bernie Sanders and later Hillary Clinton, hoping to keep Trump out of the Oval Office.

Then when Trump ran for re-election in 2020, I launched a grassroots organization that strove to rally dissatisfied Elizabeth Warren and Bernie Sanders supporters behind Joe Biden and reached tens of millions of young voters, helping propel him and Kamala Harris to victory.

Over the past few years, I worked to rally Gen Z behind Joe Biden and Kamala Harris’ groundbreaking agenda by launching _Path to Progress_.
|
---
| [ ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzQwNzEyNDQ0LFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiYjJjOTRjMjY4NTNmYjk3MyJ9)
---
|
---
But I can’t lie: **All of this wasn’t enough.**

So when Donald Trump avoided placing his hand on a Bible he’s never read and swore an oath he doesn’t mean, I’ve been thinking about what I could have done differently.**

It’s no secret that Trump and MAGA Republicans regard young people with nothing but pure disdain.** Their policies endanger our futures. Already, it is far too difficult for young Americans to go to college, start families, and hold good-paying jobs, let alone travel, work reasonable hours, and spend time with loved ones. **And yet 42% of Americans under the age of 30 voted for Donald Trump and JD Vance anyway. Why?**

It’s hard to boil down complex political phenomena into simple terms but if I had to try, I’d say Republicans made huge gains with Gen Z because they come off as authentic and uncompromising, even if young people don’t always agree with what they say.

Young people see a Democratic Party that is unwilling to stand up to special interests, unable to adequately tackle the climate crisis, and overly reluctant to hold our friends and allies accountable for their actions, both here at home and abroad. _This isn’t always the reality, but if we’ve learned anything from the 2024 election results,_it’s that perception matters just as much_._
|
---
_**[Path to Progress is the key to building a robust resistance to MAGA within Generation Z. To catalyze our movement as we head into Trump’s second term, can we count on you to make a donation of $5 or whatever you can spare?](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzQ0OTA2Nzg2LFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiODU2NzE1NDc2MWMyMWQzNCJ9)**_
|
---
| | [ Donate $5 >> ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzUwMTQ5NzAzLFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiOGI4Zjk0Yzc4MzlkNDlhMCJ9)
---
|
---
| | [ Donate another amount >> ](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzUzMjk1NDY1LFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiZGNmZTQxODkyMzNlMjQzZiJ9)
---
|
---
_**[Or multiply your impact with a monthly recurring donation](https://jjajxo.clicks.mlsend.com/tl/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyODY4MDcsXCJsXCI6MTQ0MTY5NDMxNzU3NDg5ODAxLFwiclwiOjE0NDE2OTQ1MTk2MDQwMzA1M30iLCJzIjoiN2FiYTUxNmM4ODVmOTkxNCJ9)**_
|
---
Trump’s presidency will be a long, hard road to the next presidential election and most of us will get there in one piece. But for the vulnerable among us — the uninsured, the elderly, the disabled, the undocumented, the working class, and more — we are going to be fighting against Donald Trump and his dangerous agenda at every juncture.

We want you to be there alongside us.

Thank you for your support,

Sam Weinberg,
**Executive Director**
**Path to Progress** __
|
---
|
---
|
---
|

United States of America |

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: My steadfast promise to you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
| | |
---
Friend, now that Donald Trump has begun his second term in the White House, I wanted to personally share my steadfast promise to you:
**I know that this moment and the future are filled with uncertainty. But if there's _anything_ you can count on, it's that I will wake up every single morning and fight my heart out for you and your family.**
That means taking on the issues that matter most: expanding access to affordable health care, supporting our unions, bringing down everyday costs like groceries and protecting vital federal programs like Social Security and Medicare. When it comes to all of these and more, I have a proven track record of making progress by working across the aisle, and I'm ready to do it again in the months and years ahead.
It also means holding this administration accountable and being an unrelenting force against the worst parts of their agenda. During the Senate confirmation hearings for both secretary of defense and CIA director, I spoke out about my concerns with Trump's nominees — from Pete Hegseth's treatment of women to John Ratcliffe's ability to handle intelligence that would help us prepare for a future pandemic. I will work with them to achieve our goals but won't hesitate to hold them to account when necessary over the next four years.
When it comes to the next four years, I'm ready to face whatever comes our way head-on to serve _all_ New Yorkers and _all_ Americans. And as the new chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, I'll also be laser-focused on taking back our Senate majority so we can defend working American families from this administration's extremist policies.
**I'll be honest with you: The fights ahead of us won't be easy. clean_body: | | | Friend, now that Donald Trump has begun his second term in the White House, I wanted to personally share my steadfast promise to you: I know that this moment and the future are filled with uncertainty. But if there's anything you can count on, it's that I will wake up every single morning and fight my heart out for you and your family. That means taking on the issues that matter most: expanding access to affordable health care, supporting our unions, bringing down everyday costs like groceries and protecting vital federal programs like Social Security and Medicare. When it comes to all of these and more, I have a proven track record of making progress by working across the aisle, and I'm ready to do it again in the months and years ahead. It also means holding this administration accountable and being an unrelenting force against the worst parts of their agenda. During the Senate confirmation hearings for both secretary of defense and CIA director, I spoke out about my concerns with Trump's nominees — from Pete Hegseth's treatment of women to John Ratcliffe's ability to handle intelligence that would help us prepare for a future pandemic. I will work with them to achieve our goals but won't hesitate to hold them to account when necessary over the next four years. When it comes to the next four years, I'm ready to face whatever comes our way head-on to serve all New Yorkers and all Americans. And as the new chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, I'll also be laser-focused on taking back our Senate majority so we can defend working American families from this administration's extremist policies. I'll be honest with you: The fights ahead of us won't be easy. And the only way we can win them is if we're fighting them together.= If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = With gratitude, love and fierce determination, Kirsten | * | = A note from Kirsten: Friend, I am truly honored to have won reelection, and I promise to continue the critical work toward bipartisan solutions for New Yorkers as we enter this new term. While we had many victories in New York this election season, our fight to retake control of the Senate is far from over. = Thank you, Kirsten Gillibrand

| | Yesterday’s inauguration was not the moment we hoped for. Instead of sending voting rights champions to the White House, it feels like we’re taking a step backwards. Our democracy now faces challenges unlike anything we have seen in our lifetimes. The spreading of disinformation, criminalizing parts of the voting process, and weakening voting protections are key parts of a growing anti-voter agenda. **We cannot allow this to become normalized.** Fair Fight is readying for the fights ahead, expanding what works and investing in new innovations to defend our freedom to vote and build political power across the South. We remain a trusted and crucial voice in this fight. And our power comes from this very community — from people like you. Together, we’re ready for what comes next. [**If you are able to, please make a donation today to help counter the anti-voter extremism that will come. Our fight begins today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752737/523770604/-1147768191?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250121_f_Inaug_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NmE0ZmQyYS0zNWQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7wXE36O6wnCvLzUvWdREzKWwtdOgYqAAbaCfg6__sbg=) Thank you for standing with us. name: Ilhan Omar
subject: “Give me your tired, your poor, Your huddled masses yearning to breathe free,”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1461%2E791427%2EpAh6SH)

Friend — Yesterday Donald Trump signed a flurry of executive orders that rolled back important progress with the stroke of his pen.
Throughout his week, I want to highlight some of these actions and break down exactly what this administration is doing to hurt vulnerable people and communities as they embrace far-right policies and fascist tendencies.
On the issue of immigration, Trump has signed executive orders that:
* Illegally end the constitutional right of birthright citizenship by no longer recognizing the citizenship of children born in the US to immigrants who lack legal status.

* Declare a ‘national emergency’ at the southern border that can be used to deploy the military to the border to supply troops and build mass detention camps.

* Shut down the CPD One App, a crucial resource used by migrants who are seeking asylum or legal entry into the US, and cancel all existing appointments.

* Institute a complete and indefinite ban on all refugees entering the US no matter the circumstance.

Friend — It can’t be said enough that this is not who we are. The Statue of Liberty at Ellis Island is inscribed with a simple message:
_“Give me your tired, your poor, Your huddled masses yearning to breathe free, The wretched refuse of your teeming shore. Send these, the homeless, tempest-tost to me, I lift my lamp beside the golden door!”_
As a refugee who sought asylum and immigrated to America, I know that these executive orders are a dangerous affront to our rights, our values, and the safety and prosperity of those seeking a better life in our nation.
The truth is the best solution to this crisis and the dangerous policies of Donald Trump is to rebuild our grassroots movement of progressive opposition.
Throughout our history, under presidents of both parties, grassroots movements have helped build popular support for the civil rights of Americans of all kinds and set the agenda in Washington. We saw it under Trump’s first term, and we need to bring that energy back once again.
**[So, in the wake of these new executive orders, will you pitch in $3 or whatever you can afford to help us build the movement to oppose Trump wherever we can? We can’t sit back and let our country slide into fascism. It’s time to start standing up for our immigrant friends and neighbors.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-jan25.usI49z&refcode=em250121-1-60&t=2&akid=1461%2E791427%2EpAh6SH)**

— Ilhan Omar

clean_body: Friend — Yesterday Donald Trump signed a flurry of executive orders that rolled back important progress with the stroke of his pen. Throughout his week, I want to highlight some of these actions and break down exactly what this administration is doing to hurt vulnerable people and communities as they embrace far-right policies and fascist tendencies. On the issue of immigration, Trump has signed executive orders that: Illegally end the constitutional right of birthright citizenship by no longer recognizing the citizenship of children born in the US to immigrants who lack legal status. Declare a ‘national emergency’ at the southern border that can be used to deploy the military to the border to supply troops and build mass detention camps. Shut down the CPD One App, a crucial resource used by migrants who are seeking asylum or legal entry into the US, and cancel all existing appointments. Institute a complete and indefinite ban on all refugees entering the US no matter the circumstance. Friend — It can’t be said enough that this is not who we are. The Statue of Liberty at Ellis Island is inscribed with a simple message: “Give me your tired, your poor, Your huddled masses yearning to breathe free, The wretched refuse of your teeming shore. Send these, the homeless, tempest-tost to me, I lift my lamp beside the golden door!” As a refugee who sought asylum and immigrated to America, I know that these executive orders are a dangerous affront to our rights, our values, and the safety and prosperity of those seeking a better life in our nation. The truth is the best solution to this crisis and the dangerous policies of Donald Trump is to rebuild our grassroots movement of progressive opposition. Throughout our history, under presidents of both parties, grassroots movements have helped build popular support for the civil rights of Americans of all kinds and set the agenda in Washington. We saw it under Trump’s first term, and we need to bring that energy back once again. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately: | | ** | | | | | | | We will keep tracking Trump’s executive actions and continue to keep you updated. I hope we can count on your solidarity in this challenging time. — Ilhan Omar PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS

name: Andrea Salinas
subject: what to expect from these emails going forward (please read)
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752916/523771356/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NWRlODI3Ni0zNmQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-hr6G9ZzeA6FRiuQCtWR3heWkUV-uGF-svyWdbYR9hY=) Hey, I hope you’re doing OK. I want to make something clear up front: **I do not plan to email you every single time Donald Trump says or does something outrageous**. If I did, I’d do pretty much nothing but write emails all day long, and you’d do nothing but read them (if you opened them at all, which you’d probably stop doing). But the answer isn’t disengagement or resignation either. Seeing so many harmful actions all at once feels overwhelming — but none of us can afford to throw in the towel. We still have work to do, fights to win, and _real power to determine what happens next in our country, if we choose to use it._ **So here’s what I’m going to do, and I hope you will join me here: I’m going to stay focused on what matters most, fight like hell to stop the bad things we can stop, and make progress for the American people wherever I can.** I will _never_ send you an email just to make you feel angry or scared. But I _will_ send you emails about what I’m doing to stand up for our freedoms, our democracy, and our livelihoods — and how you can get involved in this work. So, listen: if you follow the news, you probably have some idea what Donald Trump got up to on his first day in office. Among other things, he pardoned insurrectionists who violently assaulted police officers, attempted to circumvent the Constitution on birthright citizenship, attacked LGBTQ people, and made it easier for lobbyists to give gifts to government officials. **You know what he didn’t get up to? A single damn thing to make life more affordable for working families.** I’m going to make sure my constituents in OR-06 get that message loud and clear. I’m going to fight for accountability for an administration that acts like it’s above the people it serves. And I’m going to do everything I can to flip the House blue in 2026 — so that we restore Congress as a separate and co-equal branch of government with the backbone to stand up to Trump when he does things that will harm the American people. [_**I hope you will join me in this work, because I can’t do it alone. These are tough fights, but it will take all of us — and in the end, we’ll be grateful for the wins we fought for and glad we stayed engaged when we could have given up instead. So if you’re with me, I’m humbly asking you to pitch in $5 to power our work going forward, because we’ve got a lot to do.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752917/523771365/-533866197?refcode=email_012125_Trump&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NWRlODI3Ni0zNmQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-hr6G9ZzeA6FRiuQCtWR3heWkUV-uGF-svyWdbYR9hY=) | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752918/523771373/-533866197?refcode=email_012125_Trump&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NWRlODI3Ni0zNmQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-hr6G9ZzeA6FRiuQCtWR3heWkUV-uGF-svyWdbYR9hY=)

name: Mark Takano
subject: On the first full day of Trump's 2nd term
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mark Takano For Congress P
body:
| Peter, I just wanted to check in today and make sure you were doing okay.

I know that many of us have been dreading this day ever since Trump secured enough votes to secure his second term.

Just know that I am _not going anywhere_.

My Democratic colleagues are _not going anywhere_.

**And we’re going to make the next four years of Donald Trump’s presidency as painful as we can possibly make them.**

If we want to force Donald Trump to scratch and claw his way towards getting _anything_ done, then we’ll also be forced to scratch and claw our way back into power. **So needless to say, Peter – I need your help.**

My team has already started building the resources necessary to outwork Donald Trump and his MAGA network at the ballot box, on the airwaves, and online.

We need to flip critical special election seats, but until then, we need to do everything we can to spread our positive, people-first message to all Americans.

[**But I need your help to do that, Peter — can you chip in $7 to make our efforts easier and do your part to re-take power from Donald Trump and limit his MAGA destruction? »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752973/523771466/312147918?amount=7&refcode=MTFC_20250121_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTkyZDk3ZC0zNmQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yQdXA_VOSNWJPOWlk6Ss3BJGSoaV0WOUgwXad5aGjxc=) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100752979/523771472/312147918?amount=7&amounts=3,7,10&express_lane=true&refcode=MTFC_20250121_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM2MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTkyZDk3ZC0zNmQ4LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yQdXA_VOSNWJPOWlk6Ss3BJGSoaV0WOUgwXad5aGjxc=)
